import regex as re  # use regex module for arabic handling

# build normalization
AR_DIAC = re.compile(r'[\p{Mn}\u0640]')  # Mn and tatweel
PUNCT = re.compile(r'[^\p{L}\p{N}\s]')

def normalize_ar(s):
    if s is None: 
        return ''
    s = str(s)
    # unify hamza forms: alif with hamza above/below -> alif; madda -> alif
    s = s.replace('أ','ا').replace('إ','ا').replace('آ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ٱ','ا')
    s = s.replace('ؤ','و').replace('ئ','ي').replace('ة','ه')
    s = s.replace('ى','ي')
    # remove diacritics & tatweel
    s = AR_DIAC.sub('', unicodedata.normalize('NFKD', s))
    # remove punctuation
    s = PUNCT.sub(' ', s)
    # collapse spaces and lowercase 
    s = ' '.join(s.split()).lower()
    return s

def diacritics_removed(s):
    return AR_DIAC.sub('', unicodedata.normalize('NFKD', s))

def ratio(a, b):
    return difflib.SequenceMatcher(a=a, b=b).ratio()

full_span = "أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ، اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ وَاعْبُدُوا مَنْ لَمْ يَكُنْ يَعْبُدُهُ أَحَدٌ قَبْلَكُمْ، وَكُلُّ شَيْءٍ يَعْبُدُهُ اللَّـهُ، وَإِنَّمَا الْعِبَادَةُ مَا أَحَبَّ اللَّهُ أَنْ يُعْبَدَ"

nf_full

nf_full = normalize_ar(full_span)

    # check overlap: we can compute ratio or token F1
    r = ratio(nf_full, nf_text)
    if 'ايها الناس' in nf_text or 'اعبدوا الله' in nf_text or 'العباده ما احب الله' in nf_text:
        # compute additional heuristics like presence of two tokens
        shared = sum(1 for tok in nf_full.split() if tok in nf_text)
        matches.append((r, shared, h['hadithID'], text[:200], text))
# sort by ratio desc then shared

# Search quran for "يا أيها الناس اعبدوا ربكم"

norm_p = normalize_ar(p)

    t = v.get('ayah_text','')

        c.append(v)

orig = normalize_ar(full_span)
cand = normalize_ar(c[0]['ayah_text'])
print(orig)
print(cand)

# compute token F1
orig_tokens = orig.split()
cand_tokens = cand.split()
orig_set = set(orig_tokens)
cand_set = set(cand_tokens)
prec = len(orig_set & cand_set) / len(cand_set)
rec = len(orig_set & cand_set) / len(orig_set)
f1 = 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec) if prec+rec>0 else 0
ratio_val = ratio(orig, cand)
# longest common subsequence length ratio
def lcs_length(a, b):
    # dynamic programming; smaller strings
    n, m = len(a), len(b)
    dp = [0]*(m+1)
    for i, ca in enumerate(a, 1):
        prev = 0
        for j, cb in enumerate(b, 1):
            tmp = dp[j]
            dp[j] = prev+1 if ca==cb else max(dp[j], dp[j-1])
            prev = tmp
    return dp[m]
lcs_norm = lcs_length(orig, cand)/max(len(orig), len(cand))
orig, cand, len(orig), len(cand), f1, ratio_val, lcs_norm
